DOCUMENT.source-infobase-title: The Rules of the City of New York

RECORD.PARA: 1.Unless otherwise indicated, all citations are to the New York City Administrative Code. [ALP S-001]

2.Sections marked with an asterisk (*) indicate that the conduct or activity is subject to an aggravated penalty for excess profit, equal to twice the amount of money saved by respondent as a result of its failure to comply. When an aggravated penalty is sought, it will be indicated on the face of the summons and no stipulation will be offered. The respondent will be required to appear. [ALP S-001]

5.Except in connection with violations of § 24-163, a second or third offense means a violation of any section of the Air Code by the same respondent within two years of the date of occurrence of the prior violation, at the same premises (if premises-related), and involving the same equipment. In connection with violations of § 24-163, a second or third or subsequent offense is a violation by the same respondent within three years of the date of occurrence of the prior violation(s) and involving the same vehicle, where the prior violation(s) was for a violation of § 24-163. [ALP S-084]

6."(Mitigation: 01)" A zero penalty may be imposed for a first offense following a respondent's timely submission to DEP (within 45 days of the return date indicated on the Notice of Violation) of acceptable certification of compliance and admission of liability. [ALP S-001]
7.Each summons shall provide that a respondent may admit to such violation and pay the corresponding penalty amount set forth in 15 RCNY § 43-02. [ALP S-105]
(Added City Record 4/11/2016, eff. 5/11/2016; amended City Record 3/27/2023, eff. 4/26/2023; amended City Record 12/16/2024, eff. 1/15/2025) [ALP S-105]

When a respondent is found in violation of any of the following provisions of the New York City Administrative Code, Rules of the City of New York, or New York Codes, Rules and Regulations, any civil penalties imposed by a Hearing Officer under 48 RCNY § 6-17(a) and/or any default penalties imposed under 48 RCNY § 6-20(b) in accordance with Section 1049-a(d)(1)(d) of the Charter, and/or any civil penalties imposed for admissions of violation(s) under 48 RCNY § 6-09(a) or late admissions under 48 RCNY § 3-17, will be imposed in accordance with the following penalty schedule: [ALP S-014]

COL.PARA: Failure to allow DEP to obtain samples [ALP S-044]
Location of key to boiler room not posted or no key on premises
Unregistered boiler or water heater- 350,000 - 4,200,000 btu/hr
Unregistered boilers or water heaters- aggregate 350,000+ btu/hr
Unregistered fuel burning equipment or portable equipment- 350,000 - 4,200,000 btu/hr
Unregistered engine used at construction site - 50 hp +
Unregistered equipment with environment al rating of C -100 - 2,000 cfm
Unregistered equipment- all other emission sources
Failure to notify regarding change in registration information
Operating emission source with expired registration
Failure to cancel existing registration upon registration of new equipment
Failure to notify and cancel registration when removing registered equipment
Interference with or obstruction of DEP personnel
Making / filing false or misleading statements / documents
Permit, certificate, or registration not displayed
Post permit, certificate, or registration - forthwith
Installation of refuse burning equipment other than permitted types
Equipment installed / altered without work permit
Operating permitted equipment without operating certificate
Operating fuel burning equipment without operating certificate
Operating process equipment without operating certificate
Operating portable equipment without operating certificate
Operating permitted types of refuse burning equipment without operating certificate
Operating without renewal of expired operating certificate
File for renewal of operating certificate - 30 days
Failure to comply with conditions of work permit or operating certificate
Spraying asbestos onto building or structure during construction, alteration or repair
Emission of air contaminant from motor vehicle
Prevent particulates from becoming airborne - forthwith
Keep dust from becoming airborne - forthwith
Keep dust from becoming airborne - forthwith
Failure to take precautions to prevent dust from lots covered by ZR 12-10
Take precautions to prevent dust from becoming airborne - forthwith
Spraying of insulation material without proper required precaution
Take proper precautions for spraying - forthwith
Failure to take required precautions during demolition
Take proper precautions for demolition - forthwith
Emission of nitrogen oxides from boiler
Reduce nitrogen oxides emissions to legal limits - forthwith
Stop use of prohibited coating - forthwith
Causing or permitting air contaminant from open fire
Stop use of open fire - forthwith
Burning improper fuel in outdoor wood boiler
Stop use of improper fuel - forthwith
Impermissible smoke emission from outdoor wood boiler
Improperly operating outdoor wood boiler under 250,000 btu/hr
Stop improper operation of boiler - forthwith
Operating outdoor wood boiler over 250,000 btu/hr
Operating fireplace as primary source of heat
Operating new fireplace with improper fuel
Operating existing fireplace with improper firewood
Operating fireplace not in compliance with federal emissions standards
Operating wood burning heater as primary source of heat
Operating wood burning heater with non- renewable fuel
Stop operating heater with improper fuel - forthwith
Operating commercial char broiler to cook 875+ lbs of meat without emissions control
Failure to keep maintenance records for commercial char broiler
Failure to maintain records showing amount of meat cooked / purchased for commercial char broiler
Operating cookstove without emission control device
Operating stationary generator not equipped with Tier 4 engine
Concealing or masking of air contaminant emission
Undo concealment or masking of air contaminant - 30 days
Air contaminant emission exceeding environment al rating
Reduce air contaminant level as per environment al rating - forthwith
Mold assessment, abatement or remediation for a project performed by a covered person or an unlicensed person [ALP S-056]
Cause such work to be performed by an authorized person
Failure to file notice of mold project as required [ALP S-044]
Use of fuel burning equipment without required apparatus
Use required apparatus while operating equipment - forthwith
Use of fuel burning equipment by person without certificate of fitness
Operate equipment with qualified personnel - forthwith
No air contaminant recorder for boiler
Install and use air contaminant recorder - forthwith
Operating fuel burning equipment without certificate of instruction
Idling of motor vehicle engine more than three minutes
Idling of motor vehicle engine more than one minute while adjacent to school
Failure to use ultra low sulfur diesel fuel in non- road vehicle
Use low sulfur fuel in non-road vehicle - forthwith
Failure to use best available technology for emission reduction in non-road vehicle
Failure to comply with solid waste / recyclable contract spec to use ultra low sulfur diesel fuel (ULSDF)
Failure to comply with solid waste / recyclable contract spec to use best available retrofit technology (BART)
Failure to use best available retrofit technology for sightseeing bus
Failure to use ultra low sulfur diesel fuel in school bus
Failure to use best available retrofit technology in school bus as per schedule
Failure to use ultra low sulfur diesel fuel in generator for film/TV/Ad/ street fair
Failure to retrofit diesel school bus with crankcase ventilation system
Retrofit bus with crankcase ventilation system - forthwith
Using diesel school bus older than16 years to fulfill school bus contract
Stop use of buses older than 16 years - forthwith
Failure to replace retired school bus with low- emission school bus
Replace retired school bus with compliant bus - forthwith
Failure to use best available retrofit technology in heavy duty trade waste hauling vehicle as of 1/1/2020

PARA.PARAFORMAT: 10,000/ vehicle in violation (mitigation:0 1 if correction certified within 60 days) (separate penalty of 500/day for each day not corrected after 60 days)

COL.PARA: Operating soot blower on vessel within city waters
Failure to use air contaminant detector/ recording as required
Use of inadequate combustion shut-off device
Use properly functioning shutoff device - forthwith
Improper use of equipment or apparatus
Use equipment as per code - forthwith
Use of improper fuel in fuel burning equipment
Use fuel specified for equipment - forthwith
Use or delivery of heating oil containing less than 2% biodiesel
Use or deliver heating oil with at least 2% biodiesel - forthwith
Use/purchase/sale/offer/storage/transport of fuel with excess sulfur content (#2 oil)
Purchase and use fuel with proper sulfur content - forthwith
Use/purchase/sale/offer/storage/transport of fuel with excess sulfur content (#4 oil)
Purchase and use fuel with proper sulfur content - forthwith
Use of coal in fuel burning equipment
Failure to report shipment of fuel to NYC as required
Failure to have required statement on fuel information ticket
Supply required info on fuel tickets - forthwith
Failure to retain fuel information tickets by shipper
Failure to retain fuel records for one year
Retain fuel info tickets for one year - forthwith
Miscellaneous violation of Air Pollution Control Code
Failure to retain records related to combustion efficiency testing
Failure to maintain records of hours of operation for emergency generator
No stage 1 vapor collection system at gas dispensing site
Install stage 1 vapor collection system - forthwith
Failure to maintain stage 1 vapor collection and control system
Properly maintain stage 1 vapor collection/control system at site - forthwith
Failure to connect and operate vapor collection system loading and unloading at site
Connect and operate vapor collection system while loading / unloading at site - forthwith
No stage 1 vapor recovery system capacity at least 250 gallons
Install stage 1 vapor recovery system at gas dispensing site - forthwith
No certificate of registration for gasoline dispensing site
Obtain certificate of registration for gas dispensing site - forthwith
No NYS DEC date for pressure test on gasoline transporting vehicle
Post NYS DEC marking indicating last date pressure / vacuum test - forthwith
Failure to keep dome covers closed on gas transport vehicle
Keep dome cover closed while transport vehicle loading / unloading motion - forthwith
No stage 1 vapor collection / control system on gasoline transport vehicle
Install stage 1 vapor collection and control system on gas transport - forthwith
Failure to provide adequate training for operator of gasoline transport vehicle
Provide training for operator of gas transport vehicle - forthwith
Failure to maintain stage 1 vapor collection/control system on gas transport vehicle
Properly maintain stage 1 vapor collection and control system on gas transport vehicle - forthwith
Failure to connect/operate vapor collection / control system on gas transport vehicle
Connect and operate vapor collection / control system on gas transport vehicle - forthwith
Failure to control visible leaks from vehicle vapor collection/control system
Control visible leaks from vapor collection and control system loading / unloading vehicle - forthwith
Installation of perc dry cleaning machine in residential building after 7/13/06
Failure to install vapor barrier or room enclosure and general exhaust ventilation
Install vapor barrier or room enclosure and general exhaust ventilation - 30 days
Improperly installed vapor barrier or room enclosure and general exhaust ventilation
Install proper vapor barrier or room enclosure and general exhaust ventilation - 30 days
Failure to properly operate exhaust damper
Close or repair exhaust damper - forthwith
Failure to have proper emission control systems
Failure to remove equipment from service
Failure to properly replace, retrofit, or convert equipment
Replace, retrofit, or convert equipment - 30 days
Conduct inspection or self- monitoring - forthwith
Failure to repair leak within 24 hours
Failure to comply with Operations and Maintenance (O&M) requirements
Failure to have proper spill control equipment
Failure to keep solvent containers closed
Failure to properly manage hazardous waste
Failure to install proper spill containment system
Install proper spill containment system - 30 days
Failure to report release, fire or explosion
Failure to record emergency response actions
Failure to have owner or manager certification
Failure to attend required NYS DEC training and hold valid NYS DEC certificate
Failure to submit notice of alteration or modification s
Failure to have work permit or operating certificate
File for work permit or operating certificate - 14 days
Failure to have third party inspection
Failure to post New York State perc notice in easily visible location
Post notice in a conspicuous location - forthwith
Failure to post perc notice in easily visible location
Failure to post notice regarding non-perc chemical in easily visible location
Failure to post perc and non-perc notices
Violation of perc dry cleaner rules
Comply with perc dry cleaner rule - forthwith
Failure to control release of dust from construction by wetting or other acceptable means
Failure to cover trucks used to transport particulate matter
Failure to provide adequate water to perform wet method of dust control
Failure to provide suitable drainage for water and sludge
Failure to wet (and maintain wet) all exterior building surfaces prior to and during demolition
Failure to wet and/or cover construction materials before and during loading and transport
Failure to use wetting to control dust during drilling, grinding or other similar construction activities
Failure to control dust produced at transfer points
Failure to have sprinklers at transfer points capable of being operated by person responsible for loading
Failure to moisten soil or debris piles to prevent windblown dust
Failure to properly remove debris during hand demolition
Failure to board up windows to prevent dust emission during renovation
Failure to suppress dust during sandblasting
Failure to cover trucks used to transport dust- producing materials
Failure to remove earth or other materials daily
Use of blowers for mud or dirt removal
Failure to operate construction vehicles slowly to minimize dust emissions
Failure to properly stabilize disturbed areas
Failure to wet adequately before and during demolition
Failure to use chutes / buckets to transport debris
Failure to remove dust / debris daily from adjacent areas
Failure to use containment during sandblasting
Failure to use curtains during sandblasting
Failure to give required advance notice of sandblasting
Failure to register dust-emitting construction equipment with DEP
Failure to prevent dust emission from open areas after demolition

RECORD.PARA: (Added City Record 4/11/2016, eff. 5/11/2016; amended City Record 8/17/2016, eff. 9/16/2016; amended City Record 5/8/2017, eff. 6/7/2017; amended City Record 11/25/2019, eff. 12/30/2019; amended City Record 11/18/2020, eff. 12/18/2020; amended City Record 3/27/2023, eff. 4/26/2023; amended City Record 12/16/2024, eff. 1/15/2025) [ALP S-105]
